Full depth per screener: what the daily digest doesn't show. Each block carries real backtest numbers, a data-confidence sizing scale (a guide, never a permission), an AI read of today's batch, and the 90-day tape.

Macro regimeNEUTRAL (60/100)
Page built2026-06-13 14:11
How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-12 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Detects tickers appearing in 2+ coherent screeners within one trading day; edge comes from signal stacking cutting false-positive rate.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead
SMART_FLOW × UNUSUAL_V3FULL SIZEN=168 · DSR +4.78 · promoted VALID by the May-2026 adversarial batteryThe one stock+flow pair that survived everything. Cabled in best_trades.
Any 3+ VALID screeners within ±1 dayFULL SIZEWR 81.7% · Sharpe 2.68 · p_bonf < 0.0001 (N=126, bucket-level)The bucket edge: triple agreement among validated screeners beats solos by +5.8pp WR.
UNUSUAL_V3 × end-of-month (last 3 bd)FULL SIZESharpe +5.48 · p_bonf 0.0096 (STRICT_VALID calendar combo)Cabled ×1.5 in best_trades when a UV3 signal fires in the last 3 business days of the month.
BOTTOM × DEAD sectorHALF SIZEtrain Sharpe +2.73 → test +1.93, ROBUST OOS (sector-context combo)Contrarian: bottoms inside left-for-dead sectors. Cabled ×1.2 in best_trades.
PULLBACK × NEW_DOWN sectorHALF SIZEtrain Sharpe +2.84 → test +1.25, ROBUST OOS (sector-context combo)Cabled ×1.2 in best_trades.
BOTTOM × SMART_FLOWSTARTER · edge unproventhe 'magic combo' (Sharpe 6.17, N=71) was NOISE: DSR -1.33, temporal clusteringKept here as a warning. Do not size this overlap on the old headline numbers.

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

IREN leads at 164 on BREAKOUT, CRYPTO_ETF, and UNUSUAL, a crypto-adjacent stack that fits risk-on positioning but carries thematic concentration risk. AMAT scores 156 across four screeners - BLOCK_TRADERS, CLENOW_MOMENTUM, MOMENTUM_FACTOR, and VOLUME_LEADER - representing the cleanest broad-based momentum and flow alignment in the batch. ORCL at 140 adds a vol-skew and Clenow momentum angle worth watching for a post-earnings drift setup. The distribution is top-heavy: three names cluster between 140 and 164 while the remaining seven compress between 82 and 127, and score_stats provides only n_overlaps=20 with no mean or dispersion data, so granular rank ordering below the top three is unreliable. Critically, no name today matched a validated combo, meaning none of the tested VALID or MARGINAL pairings fired, and macro sits at a soft NEUTRAL 60.11 with no regime tailwind. Lead with AMAT at moderate conviction - four coherent non-overlapping screener types with no mixed signals - and treat IREN as a secondary idea only if crypto sentiment is running.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · block-bootstrap · Sharpe 2.63 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=212 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(block) 0.000 · DSR 1.00 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta direction, filtered through EMA8/21 trend structure for momentum alignment.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $EFA $VG
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1EFA ↑55 Watch$104.61 -0.11%BULLISH SWEEPCALL107$172KNEUTRAL0100STRONG1010
2VG ↑50 Watch$12.74 -0.08%BULLISH SWEEPCALL12.5$241KOPENING929076BROKEN44
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking is statistically meaningless at four resolved trades out of 271 total, so the 100% win rate and 200% average versus SPY's 5.25% cannot confirm or contradict the backtest Sharpe of 2.63 - the key number is the 4-resolved figure, which makes the WATCH verdict the only honest call.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (4 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 271 signals captured · 4 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +5.2%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 269 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin and low-conviction: two Watch-tier signals, EFA at 55 and VG at 50, both flagged as BULLISH SWEEP but neither clears the 65 threshold where the backtest edge concentrates. EFA is the more liquid name and a bullish sweep on an international equity ETF carries some macro read-through interest in a NEUTRAL regime, but a score of 55 with no signals above 65 in a two-name batch is not a setup to chase. Score distribution is minimal, top-heavy compression is absent, and the macro regime at 60 does not provide a tailwind. If forced to rank, EFA first, but conviction is low - size accordingly and wait for a score reset above 65 before committing real capital.

🧠 Ranks variance premium-selling candidates by the IV30 minus HV30 spread, sector-adjusted per Sinclair's table, to capture structural options overpricing.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms rather than contradicts the backtest edge, with 57% win rate and +6.7% average beating SPY by 4.0 points, and the May 2026 cohort holds at 57% WR and +7.5% average, the key number being no degradation month over month.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE scores 81, the only name above 80 in this 39-signal batch, and sits squarely in the Utilities sector where Sinclair's model applies a structural +31% premium bias, making it the clear standout. D at 77 and ES at 76 are the next tier, both Utilities, both Signal-grade. Score distribution is compressed: 37 of 39 names cluster between 65 and 80, so this is a mid-tier batch with no broad conviction sweep below the top name. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (60.11), neither a headwind nor a tailwind for premium selling. No pattern data exists on any name today. Start with NEE first at low-to-moderate conviction given the tight clustering, and pass on the pile of 75-scored Utilities without a clear differentiator.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.13 productization of VARIANCE_PREMIUM MID tertile: OOS ROBUST (test Sharpe 2.13 vs train 1.53, decay 1.39), 2y Polygon options backfill Full methodology →
🧠 Captures tickers where IV sits in the middle tertile of variance risk premium, signaling options are fairly priced relative to realized vol without crowded hedging distortion.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

BBY and IHI both score a perfect 100 in the Very Strong tier, making them the lead names: BBY offers a single-name consumer discretionary entry with options priced cleanly, while IHI gives medtech exposure via ETF structure with the same fair-value vol read. BITU and MOD follow at 99, BITU being a leveraged crypto proxy that warrants sizing caution despite the score. The distribution is top-heavy, with 26 of 50 signals at 80 or above and 43 clearing 65, meaning the screener is crowded toward conviction rather than spread thin. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 neither adds tailwind nor blocks the thesis, so entries are viable but not turbocharged. First preference is IHI on highest score with lowest single-name event risk, moderate conviction.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

With 44 of 50 names scoring exactly 100 and the median pinned at the ceiling, this batch offers no internal ranking - every name is tied, pattern fields are null across the board, and differentiation is zero. Among the top eight, AER stands out as a high-beta industrial with clean leasing cash flows that often attracts macro hedgers, AME carries steady AMETEK compounder characteristics that fit a neutral-macro tape, and AMKR is the highest-beta name in the set given its semiconductor packaging leverage to AI hardware demand. The score distribution is not broad or top-heavy in the useful sense - it is compressed into a single bin, which means the screener has hit a saturation event and rank-ordering requires looking outside this output. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 neither confirms nor blocks the thesis, but it mutes conviction. If forced to act, take AME first as the lowest-volatility expression of the signal at moderate conviction, and treat AMKR as a higher-risk secondary only with tight sizing.

Flags Stage 4 names (below declining MA30W) with deep 90d drawdown + washed-out RSI as contrarian basket candidates: the Weinstein backtest inverted, Stage 4 entries mean-revert +11.25% SPY-relative at 120d.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 3 closed · WR TB 0% · 0% expired · sample too small to read (N<10)
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AVTX and BKKT share the top score at 91 (Very strong) but carry heavy speculative weight - AVTX is a small biotech and BKKT is crypto-adjacent, with no pattern confirmation on either name. BABA at 87 is the only pick here with institutional liquidity and a genuine multi-year drawdown narrative that fits the mean-reversion thesis. Score distribution is top-heavy: median 82, 18 of 27 names clear the 80 threshold, all 27 clear 65, so the batch is uniformly elevated but lacks true outlier separation between the top tier and the rest. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) provides no directional tailwind and does not impair the basket logic, but it gives no reason to press size. Prefer BABA first as a LEAPS position at 1-2% of basket, moderate conviction given the absence of pattern data across every pick today.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol exhaustion by combining VVIX percentile, VIX term structure inversion, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag sell-volatility entries at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑66● Signal$738.37 +0.08%SELL VOL1.95
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 14 resolved trades the verdict is formally WATCH, and the headline numbers back that caution: the 71.4% win rate sounds solid but the average 20-day gain of 2.69% barely clears SPY's own 2.66% over the same windows, making the edge near-zero in absolute terms, while the Signal-tier subset at 100% WR and 3.73% average across just 5 trades and a step-down from April's 5.43% average to May's 2.16% suggests the cohort tailwind is fading.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, SPY at a score of 66, sitting at the exact floor of the Signal tier with no pattern attached and nothing above 80. The score distribution is a single point, not a distribution at all. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 60.11, which neither supports nor contradicts a vol-climax read, but the absence of any corroborating breadth across other tickers weakens the case. SPY is the only actionable name and only at low conviction; size accordingly and wait for a higher-scoring reload or a second confirming signal before pressing.

🧠 Detects outsized options activity (5x 20d volume, $50K+ premium, Vol/OI above 5) filtered to calm, non-extended stocks to isolate informed pre-move positioning.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 100% win rate and 183.95% avg return against a 2.17% SPY baseline, but with only 17 resolved trades against 620 total the sample is far too thin to confirm or contradict the marginal backtest, and the verdict_detail flags this explicitly as insufficient data.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

2026-05293100%+4562.2%+56644.0%35.3%
2026-04571100%+5455.3%+102056.0%164.6%
2026-03184100%+5416.7%+45278.9%461.3%
🧠 AI read · today's batch

BTDR leads at 78 with a clean bullish sweep matching its LONG tag, making it the only top-five name without a direction-pattern conflict. MARA (77), WULF (76), XOVR (74), and IREN (72) all carry a LONG direction but a BEARISH SWEEP pattern, a divergence that typically signals protective put buying or dealer hedging rather than directional conviction, and that cluster is all crypto miners, which concentrates sector risk sharply. UVXY at 65 with a bullish sweep is a separate signal entirely, suggesting someone is adding volatility exposure, consistent with a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60) that offers no tailwind for either directional or vol bets. Score distribution is compressed, max 78 with nothing above 80 and a median of 57, so the batch is thin at the top rather than broadly strong. First preference is BTDR at moderate conviction given signal-direction alignment, hold on the miner cluster until the sweep direction resolves.

🧠 Ranks 11 GICS sector ETFs by strength, acceleration, and RS crossover vs SPY to flag early entry and sector exhaustion.
Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLB and XLU both print 95 (Very strong LONG), with XLRE at 85 (Strong), making the dominant signal a defensive/rate-sensitive rotation into Materials, Utilities, and Real Estate. The score distribution is sharply top-heavy: three names at 85-95, then a gap to XLE at 60, then four names bunched at 50-55 with no pattern tag on any ticker. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 is an awkward backdrop for a defensive rotation read, neither validating a risk-off bid nor supporting a broad momentum chase. XLB is the first preference over XLU: Materials can carry both a defensive and a reflation narrative, adding flexibility; conviction is moderate given absent pattern tags and thin backtest data.

🧠 Identifies stocks down 40 percent or more from their 52-week high with capitulation volume and a reversal candle, scoring the setup on drawdown depth, ATR contraction, and volume spike.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape at 48 percent win rate contradicts the backtest's 63 percent, but the +3.9 percent average gain against SPY's -0.4 percent confirms the screener still generates real alpha, with the Signal tier's 64.6 percent win rate and +11.3 percent average being the single most important number, since it outperforms both the Very Strong and Strong tiers on a risk-adjusted basis. Monthly cohorts reinforce the improving trend, rising from a -9.5 percent average in January to +5.9 percent in May, which is consistent with regime normalization rather than mean-reversion decay.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEWR 48% · avg +3.9% ≈ SPY -0.4% (+4.4 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 48% · avg +3.9% · vs SPY -0.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 491 closed · WR TB 31% · 9% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ADCT and VRRM both print perfect 100 scores in the Very Strong tier with breakout patterns, making them the lead names today. BILI at 95 adds a liquid China internet vehicle to the same breakout setup. The score distribution is top-heavy and unusually compressed: median sits at 81, all 50 signals clear 65, and 26 of 50 clear 80, which means there is no weak tail diluting the batch. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 offers no directional tailwind, so these are stock-specific bounces rather than a broad mean-reversion wave. Take ADCT first at moderate conviction, sized to the 90-day Very Strong tier's 50 percent win rate but +28 percent average gain, which reflects fat right-tail payoffs rather than frequency.

Construction
Fresh 52w high or pivot breakout after tight base contraction (ATR shrinking). Score uses breakout strength, volume confirmation, weekly trend, and SPY filter (require_spy_above_sma50).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape (WR 53%, avg +8.5%, beating SPY by 5.5 points, KEEP verdict) broadly confirms the backtest edge, but the entire margin sits in the Very strong and Strong tiers (66-74% WR, +12 to +26% avg) while Watch names drag the composite down to near zero, making tier discipline the single number that determines whether this screener pays.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MUU is the only name firing at full strength (score 100, Very strong tier) and is the clear first look. AGL follows at 86 (Strong tier) and is the only other name with live-data tier support worth touching. After those two, the list falls off a cliff: BEZ, HIMZ, AMDL, SENS, SOXL, and HYMC all land in Watch territory between 36 and 39, where the 90-day data shows a 33.8% win rate and a mean return of -4.9%. The score distribution is extremely top-heavy, with a median of 15 across 41 signals and only 2 names clearing 80. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 does not actively support breakout momentum, so sizing should reflect that. Lead with MUU at moderate conviction; AGL is a secondary add-on only if MUU confirms intraday.

Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape flatly contradicts the backtest: the 90-day win rate of 47% undercuts the backtest's 65%, and the Very strong tier - the only tier in today's batch - is the worst performer on record at 0% wins and -24.7% average return across 6 resolved trades, while the monthly cohort trend shows a clear decay from March's 66.8% win rate and +4.9% avg down to May's 42.8% and +0.5%.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RMAX scores a perfect 100 and INTC hits 95, both flagged as LAUNCH pattern in the Very strong tier, making them the headline names. The score distribution is entirely compressed at the top: all 50 signals land at 80 or above with a median of 85, meaning the screener offers no differentiation within today's batch. That compression is a warning, not a green light. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (60.11), which neither supports nor blocks the setup. The critical problem is that the Very strong tier has a 0% win rate and a mean return of -24.7% in the 90-day live window, which is the exact tier every pick today sits in. Given that, INTC is the only name here with sufficient liquidity and options market depth to size cautiously if forced to act, but conviction is low.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by trend quality (exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days), gated by price above MA100 and SPY above MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

Data confidence HALF SIZE · block-bootstrap · Sharpe 0.03 · N=7294 · p(block) 0.034 · DSR 0.96 INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades Full methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breaks below VAL or above VAH that reverse next session, trapping directional momentum traders on the wrong side.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AEE $APD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AEE ↑55 Watch$109.64 +1.28%BEAR_TRAP108.18111.482.08
2APD ↑55 Watch$282.40 +1.54%BEAR_TRAP278.03281.426.18
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Zero resolved trades out of 56 total in the 90-day window means there is no live performance data to validate or challenge the backtest's marginal 0.376% mean PnL and 0.025 Sharpe, and the tie-break win rate of 37.5% across 16 expired positions is the only live number available, which leans bearish on the edge.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects stocks continuing to drift higher after a positive EPS surprise gap, exploiting slow institutional absorption of earnings revisions over 1 to 3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.

11ADSK ↑50 Watch$200.72 -2.36%5.2%4-14.7%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape (WR 56%, avg +2.4%, beating SPY -0.2% by 2.6 points) confirms the short-hold edge and supports the KEEP verdict, but May's cohort slipped to 54.4% WR and +1.9% avg on 160 signals, suggesting mild return compression as the tape has been choppier, consistent with the backtest's MARGINAL rather than robust classification.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

APPS leads the batch at 85 (Strong, PRIMED) and is the clearest candidate given its score separation from the field. BBCP and BKE both score 80 (Strong, PRIMED), forming a tight top cluster of three names above the 80 threshold. The distribution is mildly top-heavy: 3 of 12 signals at or above 80, median at 70, with 7 clearing 65, which is a decent but not exceptional batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) provides no directional lift, and the screener's validated edge only holds at the 5-day horizon, so the short-hold constraint is strict. One wrinkle from the 90-day tier data: Signal-tier names have historically produced the highest avg return in this screener (5.1%), so ANF and ASAN at 75 deserve attention alongside the headline names. Start with APPS at moderate conviction, 5-day hold, sizing down relative to a confirmed-regime environment.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Price ≥ $5, volume ≥ 500K, market cap ≥ $500M
  • Fixed 5-day hold
Score
min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Two signals today, CIEN and HPE, both scoring 100 and flagged Very strong LONG. The score distribution is as thin and top-heavy as it gets: two names, both pinned at the ceiling, with no pattern field populated for either, so the qualitative read on the setup is missing. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 offers no directional tailwind but also no active headwind. Given the identical scores, prefer CIEN over HPE at low conviction, watching for volume confirmation in the first hour, but the absence of pattern data and the screener's marginal backtest grade argue for small size.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects when 2 or more major intermarket correlation pairs break by 2 sigma or more over 7 days, then fades the dislocation with a contrarian SPY long.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

One signal today: SPY at score 95, tier Very Strong, direction LONG, pattern field null. The score distribution is as thin as it gets - one name, one score, median equals the max, no spread to characterize. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) is a poor host for an intermarket contrarian play, which needs visible stress or dislocation to generate the mean-reversion the doctrine assumes. If you take the SPY long, size it at minimal conviction - the pattern field is empty, the backtest is flagged MARGINAL with no rigorous stats, and the regime is not confirming.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score 100 with every name at maximum tier, which immediately flags a scoring ceiling problem, not a batch of 50 exceptional setups. Among names worth a second look: AFRM given its high-beta fintech exposure fits a risk-on tape, and ALB as a beaten lithium name reclaiming its 200-day carries mean-reversion momentum logic. ARKK and ARKW are also crossing, but ETF signals in a pure-price crossover screen add noise rather than alpha. The score distribution is entirely compressed at 100 with zero dispersion, so the scoring system is providing no ranking signal today. Macro is NEUTRAL at 60.11, which neither supports nor kills momentum trades. Given thin differentiation across the batch, AFRM is the first-look name at low conviction, sized accordingly, with ALB as a secondary idea if you want a sector catalyst angle.

Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day aggregate says KEEP at 50.1% win rate and +0.5% avg beating SPY by +0.4 points, but April and May cohorts both printed negative avg returns (-0.73% and -0.02%), and the tier inversion, where Watch names average +2.1% while Very Strong names average -19.5%, contradicts any thesis that higher scores here carry higher expected returns.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AI prints the day's lone 95 score at Very Strong tier with a PRIMED pattern, but the 90-day tier breakdown makes that score a warning, not a green light: Very Strong (17 trades) has a 5.9% win rate and averages -19.5%, the worst of any tier. APGE and BTDR both score 85 Strong PRIMED and sit in a tier that at least stays near flat (-0.9% avg). The 50-signal batch has a median of 70 and 12 names above 80, so it is moderately top-heavy without being especially concentrated. A NEUTRAL macro at 60.11 provides no squeeze tailwind and no headwind, leaving the setup dependent entirely on stock-specific flow. First preference is BTDR or APGE at 85 Strong PRIMED over AI, at low conviction, given the tier-level performance inversion that penalises the highest scores most severely.

Data confidence STARTER Kerberos #5: top-30 beats SPY net OOS (p=0.005-0.035) BUT survivor-biased (single membership snapshot), paper-validate Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks RUT-1000 stocks by relative volume across three lookbacks times multi-horizon price momentum, surfacing names where volume leadership confirms trend.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ALAB, MRVL, and SNOW all print 100 - the ceiling - making them the lead names from today's batch, with ALAB the most interesting given its position at the intersection of AI infrastructure and networking volume surges. CRDO and DELL at 99 round out the semiconductor and compute hardware angle, which is thematically coherent. The score distribution is extreme: all 50 signals score 80 or above, median sits at 96, meaning the screen has compressed the entire eligible universe into the top decile - that is a clustering artifact, not genuine differentiation, and reduces the signal quality of individual ranks. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 60.11, which neither supports nor kills momentum longs, but does mean you are not getting a tailwind. Given no pattern data is available for any name, conviction relies entirely on the volume-momentum composite. First preference is ALAB on the thesis that AI networking volume leadership is the most durable variant of this signal right now, but size it at reduced conviction given the distribution compression.

Data confidence STARTER Big-money footprints, old solo Sharpe ~3.4 NOT block-bootstrapped; treat as observational until rigorous run Full methodology →
🧠 Catches institutional accumulation via large OTM block trades on quiet stocks, confirmed by next-day OI delta separating new positions from closers.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.
Top picks: $SMQ $VSCO $JRE

17DECO ↑61 Watch$77.81 +5.27%4.092$0.5M
18AMWD ↑58 Watch$48.09 +12.97%7.142$48.7M
19QNXT ↑58 Watch$30.95 +2.43%10.262$0.4M
20USRD ↑58 Watch$37.39 +1.76%2.962$0.0M
21SCVL ↑56 Watch$17.43 +4.68%2.693$16.8M
22GRN ↑52 Watch$30.90 +0.88%1.591$0.1M
23FFIC ↑52 Watch$15.47 -3.13%24.453$35.9M
24NERD ↑52 Watch$19.53 -1.85%9.851$0.3M
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (55.2% WR, avg +3.6%, beating SPY by +4.5 points) and back-to-back positive monthly cohorts (April avg +9.1%, May avg +4.8%) confirm the screener is live, though the monthly average is decelerating, which is the one number worth watching.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMQ at 82 and VSCO at 81 are the only two names clearing the Strong tier, and that tier is the one that matters: 90-day tracking shows Strong running 60% win rate and avg +8.95% over 20 days, far above the Signal bucket's +1.8%. JRE and BNDD tie at 78 in Signal tier and are worth watching but carry meaningfully less expected edge by tier history. The 24-signal batch is moderately broad, with 14 of 24 names above 65 and only 2 clearing 80, so the distribution is not top-heavy but the strong end is thin. Macro at NEUTRAL (60.11) neither amplifies nor suppresses; this tape is not hostile to the signal but offers no tailwind. No pattern data is populated for any name today, so the thesis rests entirely on premium size and OI confirmation. Start with SMQ at high conviction, VSCO immediately behind it.

🧠 Detects excess high bars at the top of the value area with rejection tails of at least 1.8x ATR and location above 70 percent, flagging supply exhaustion setups for short entries.
Construction
Mirror of DALTON: excess high bars at the top of the value area, rejection tail >= 1.8x ATR, location >= 70%.
Top picks: $FEMR $DGS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %
1FEMR ↓96●●● Very strong$41.61 -0.16%HIGH1.9278.8%
2DGS ↓94●●● Very strong$65.37 +0.34%HIGH1.8787.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) in the last 3 bars
  • SHORT direction: excess on the HIGH side AND trade location ≥ 70%
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With zero resolved trades out of 11 and the only available proxy being a test-bed win rate of 33 percent on six trades, there is no live tape evidence to confirm or contradict anything - the screener is still in observation mode and the 33 percent figure is the one number that at minimum warrants caution, not confidence.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Two signals today, both rated Very Strong: FEMR at 96 and DGS at 94. Pattern field is null on both, so the specific rejection structure has to be verified on the chart before acting. The score distribution is not broad - it is a two-name batch clustered tightly between 94 and 96, which is top-heavy by definition but means nothing statistically at this sample size. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 60.11, offering no directional edge for shorts and no obvious regime headwind either. FEMR gets first look at best given the marginal score advantage, but conviction should be low until the rejection tail is confirmed on the raw bar.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects failed price auctions via Market Profile rejection tails at least 1.8x ATR wide, forming at value-area extremes, signaling mean reversion back into accepted value.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $ACB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1ACB ↑94●●● Very strong$3.11 +0.84%LOW1.8826.9%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Tracking is uninformative: 0 of 7 signals have resolved, no win rate or SPY alpha exists yet, and the backtest itself rests on N=74 without block bootstrap, so there is no tape evidence to confirm or contradict the raw +3.18% at 10 days - the key number is simply zero resolved trades.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is one name: ACB at a score of 94, tier Very Strong, directional bias LONG. The score distribution is not a distribution at all - it is a single observation, median equals max at 94, which tells you nothing about relative ranking within the day. Pattern field is null, so the qualitative setup label is unavailable. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 neither supports nor undercuts a mean-reversion long in a cannabis name, but ACB is a low-liquidity, structurally challenged equity where Market Profile signals carry additional noise risk. Conviction here is low-to-moderate: if you trade it, size it accordingly and treat it as a single-name speculative idea, not a systematic edge.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Weekly red-candle pullback then reclaim of the prior week's high within four weeks of an earnings gap, capturing PEAD continuation momentum.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $ATEX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago
1ATEX ↑86●● Strong$84.00 +3.14%5.7%3.421
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms real edge - 68% WR and +16.4% avg against SPY's +3.7% is a +12.7 point spread that is hard to dismiss - but the monthly cohort trend shows a soft deterioration, with April at 81.8% WR slipping to May at 55%, a 27-point drop worth monitoring before adding size.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, ATEX, score 86, Strong tier. No pattern metadata is attached, so the case rests entirely on the tier and score. Score distribution is trivial by definition - one signal means there is no spread to characterize, no cluster, no skew. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) neither helps nor hurts, but it does mean the entry depends on ATEX carrying its own fundamental weight rather than a rising tide. The Strong tier has the best live stats of any cohort (87.5% WR, avg +38.6% per the 90d tracking), so ATEX is the only actionable name and deserves first look at the open, with conviction calibrated to a small starter given the paper-thin batch.

Smart Flow Filtered (KVO+FORCE)

🧠 Removes noisy SMART_FLOW entries by skipping any signal when KVO cross or Force Index flip is active within a 3-day window, using volume conflict as a negative filter.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $MCHP
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1MCHP ↑50 Watch$92.88 -0.06%BULLISH SWEEPCALL105$130KNEUTRAL08555
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

One name, one signal: MCHP scores 50 (Watch tier) on a BULLISH SWEEP pattern, sitting exactly at the median, which means this is the weakest permissible read the screener can produce. Zero signals clear 65, zero clear 80. The batch is not clustered or top-heavy, it is simply empty except for a single floor-level entry. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 neither blocks nor supports the trade. Conviction here is minimal: MCHP can stay on the radar but does not warrant a primary position today.

🧠 Identifies bull traps where price breaks above the value area high then closes back inside, signaling trapped longs and a short entry.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ACWI
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ACWI ↓75● Signal$155.85 +0.01%BULL_TRAP155.42139.19
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Zero resolved trades out of 40 live signals means no confirmed edge can be measured, and the only partial read - a 31.25% win rate across 16 options trades - runs below chance and contradicts rather than supports the backtest thesis, which itself is already rejected.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

One signal today: ACWI at score 75, Signal tier, no pattern detail attached. A single name at the minimum qualifying threshold, with nothing clearing 80, is a thin batch by any measure. NEUTRAL macro at 60.11 provides no directional tailwind for a short-biased screen. The backtest on this screener carries a REJECT verdict - Sharpe of -0.001, p-Bonferroni of 1.0, and a DSR of 0.27 - meaning the theoretical edge is not established. Trade ACWI short only at the lowest possible size, and only if intraday tape confirms the trap structure; do not treat this screen output as a stand-alone signal.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Monthly cohorts directly contradict the screener edge: March 2026 printed 72% WR and plus 8.6% avg but was flagged as an artefact, and since then the series has collapsed to 44% WR in April and 41% WR in May with a near-zero avg of plus 0.2%, which matches the REJECT verdict and confirms the backtest failure was not a fluke.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LFVN is the lone score-80 TRIGGER and the only Strong-tier name today, but that tier has produced 0 wins in 8 live trades with an average loss of 28.95% against the short, making it the most dangerous slot in this screener despite the highest score. APPS, ARCB, and BBCP sit at 75 in the SOW ZONE, a more workable tier historically, with APPS and SMCI offering better liquidity among the Signal names. Score distribution is thin at the top: median of 60, only 1 name at 80, and 24 of 50 at 65 or above, meaning most of the batch is mid-grade noise. NEUTRAL macro at 60.11 gives no tailwind for mean reversion, and May cohort data shows the tape has been grinding higher against these shorts. Avoid LFVN outright; if any name here, APPS at 75 is first in line at low conviction only.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is useless for prioritization: all 50 names score 99-100, the model has hit a ceiling and cannot rank within the batch. Of the top picks, MRVL and MU are the most structurally interesting given semiconductor cycle positioning, and HIMS carries event-driven momentum from weight-loss drug tailwinds, but no pattern data exists for any name, so these reads come from sector context, not screener signal. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) neither supports nor kills a momentum tilt, but it provides no cover if the tape rolls. Given a REJECT backtest, a fully compressed score distribution, and zero pattern differentiation, this batch is weak. If forced to act, MRVL first at minimal size only.

🧠 Catches extreme weekly RSI(14) oversold stocks where daily crossover, candlestick reversal, and demethod zone scoring align for a contrarian mean-reversion bounce.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape partially contradicts the KILL verdict at the Signal tier level - 89% WR and avg +8.47% beats SPY's +7.9% across 82 resolved trades - but the aggregate is still a loser vs the index (+5.7% vs +7.9%), and monthly avg returns are decelerating sharply from March's +6.67% to May's +2.93%, which is the key number confirming the doctrine rejection holds.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NFG tops the batch at 78 with an active TRIGGER pattern, making it the only name with a confirmed entry signal worth acting on today. IVA at 75 is the only other TRIGGER, giving it second priority. The remaining six names - ALTI, CMCM, HII, UWMC, ADCT, NFLX - are all SETUP-stage at 67-70, meaning the reversal condition has not fired yet. Score distribution is weak: median at 58, zero names above 80, and only 11 of 50 clearing 65 - this is a thin, bottom-heavy batch with two names doing most of the work. NEUTRAL macro at 60.11 provides no directional tailwind for contrarian longs. First look is NFG on size, low conviction, with IVA as a secondary watch if it triggers intraday.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven mega-session 2026-05-16 crypto battery: 0/16 signals survive adversarial validation post-spot-ETF 2024 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects unusual institutional flow into crypto-linked ETFs by measuring volume deviation from mean, NAV premium, and OI delta weighted by spot crypto correlation.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $IREN $CLSK $MARA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1IREN ↑95●●● Very strong$57.99 +2.26%48.6422.9%neutral
2CLSK ↑75● Signal$16.36 +1.18%52.0241.7%neutral
3MARA ↑75● Signal$13.86 +1.86%50.3826.9%neutral
4CORZ ↑50 Watch$27.99 +2.83%58.8633.5%neutral
5RIOT ↑50 Watch$26.80 +2.51%55.9725.0%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 63% win rate is a mirage: average 20-day return is -1.4% against SPY's -0.6%, a -0.8-point gap, and the May 2026 cohort of 18 trades averaged -3.54% with WR down to 56%, confirming the KILL verdict and directly contradicting any edge implied by the headline win rate.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IREN is the only name clearing 80 (score 95, TRIGGER) and is the lone candidate worth a second look, though the screener carries a hard REJECT backtest so that look should be defensive. CLSK and MARA both land at 75 (Signal, BUY) and are mid-tier at best in a five-name batch where the bottom two names sit at 50. Score distribution is top-heavy: one outlier at 95, a cluster of two at 75, two stragglers at 50, median 75 masking a thin field. NEUTRAL macro at 60.11 provides no regime support. If forced to act, IREN first on size only, conviction low.

🧠 Identifies tickers inside theme ETFs at EMERGING or BUILDING signal status, using ETF holdings as a proxy for nascent capital flows into thematic baskets.
Construction
Detects emerging investment themes (e.g. Robotics, Space, AI Agents, Quantum). Tickers are extracted via ETF holdings of qualifying theme ETFs. Signal status: EMERGING / BUILDING / ESTABLISHED / CROWDED.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLP and ARKG both top the batch at 74, but the pairing is telling: a consumer staples defensive ETF sharing the lead with a genomics fund in an emerging-themes screener signals the model is capturing rotation noise as much as genuine thematic momentum. FDIS at 73 (consumer discretionary) and KRE at 71 (regional banks) reinforce that read, as none of these are nascent themes. HACK at 68 is the cleanest thematic candidate here, sitting in an AI-adjacent cybersecurity basket with a real structural tailwind. Score distribution is weak: no name clears 80, only 14 of 50 clear 65, and the median sits at 61, which is compressed and uninspiring. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 offers no lift. If forced to act, HACK is the first name up, low conviction, sized accordingly.

Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape is constructive on paper - 60% WR, +9.1% avg, and a +10.4 point beat over SPY at -1.3% - but the tier-level breakdown contradicts the setup because the Watch tier (+12.8% avg) is carrying the aggregate while the Very Strong tier (+2.9%) underperforms, and with monthly_cohorts empty and no backtest run, there is no way to verify whether that edge is stable or a short-window artifact.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 60% · avg +9.1% · beats SPY -1.3% by +10.4 pts
Last 90 days: 120 signals captured · 120 resolved at +20d · WR 60% · avg +9.1% · vs SPY -1.3%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 120 closed · WR TB 34% · 39% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.
Does the strength predict? (resolved signals by tier, +20d)

📈 Past cohorts (>30 days)
Not enough resolved signals yet for this screener.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names print the maximum score of 100 in the Very Strong tier: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. No pattern field is populated for any of them, so the signal is score-only with no structural context to discriminate within the group. The distribution is top-heavy, 8 of 12 Very Strong prints sitting at the ceiling, with the median across all 50 signals at 70 and 41 names clearing 65 - a broad, crowded batch rather than a sharp concentrated one. The complication is that 90-day tracking shows the Very Strong tier averaging only +2.9% over 20 days vs +12.8% for the Watch tier, meaning today's highest-rated cohort has historically been the weakest performer by avg return. Macro is NEUTRAL at 60.11, adding no directional edge. If forced to act, SRTY is the single most unusual flag - a 3x inverse Russell ETF hitting a LONG signal in a strength screener suggests the tape is pricing in small-cap deterioration, worth monitoring for a small position at low conviction pending confirmation.

🧠 Detects institutional accumulation by scoring quarterly 13F filings from 20 top hedge funds, rewarding new and increased positions.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names clearing the Very Strong tier, putting them in a class apart from the remaining 48 signals. AVGO (79) is the next name worth watching but falls just short of that threshold. The distribution is moderately top-heavy: only 2 of 50 signals reach 80 or above, 13 reach 65, and the median sits at 55, meaning most of the batch is noise. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 does not add conviction in either direction, and the structural 60-to-90-day lag in 13F data means these positions were built weeks ago. First preference is ASML at high conviction given its score gap versus the field, with FB close behind; size both positions knowing the entry signal is already stale.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and SMMT both print 100, the ceiling, making them the lead names by rank, though the absence of pattern data means the thesis rests entirely on the Form 4 signal itself with no technical confirmation. RSG at 98 is the most defensively credible name in the batch, a large-cap waste-services compounder where a C-suite buy carries real conviction weight in a NEUTRAL macro. The score distribution is completely compressed: all 50 signals score at or above 80 and the median sits at 94, which means the ranking system is providing almost no discrimination across this batch. NEUTRAL macro at 60.11 does not actively support chasing small-cap insider plays; RSG is the first preference at moderate conviction, with SVRE as a speculative second only after checking the filing size and buyer rank.

🧠 Finds stocks lagging their sector move in LATE_CHASE or RIDING sectors, scoring by sector relative strength times individual underperformance.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak. All 24 signals score exactly 50, which is simultaneously the median and the maximum, meaning the scoring distribution has zero spread and no name stands out. NHI, SVC, and CSGP are the names a sector-laggard framework would normally surface first given REIT and real-estate data exposure, but every ticker here carries the same Watch tier and null pattern, so there is no technical confirmation to lean on. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 60.11, which does not actively support catch-up momentum plays. Conviction is low across the board and the right action today is to pass, not force a name.

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs by Antonacci dual momentum, requiring positive 12-month absolute return and top relative rank versus peers to go long.
Construction
Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

EEM leads at score 90 (Very strong), the only name clearing that threshold, signaling emerging markets are outperforming on both absolute and relative momentum legs. IWM (86, Strong) and QQQ (82, Strong) are close behind, meaning small-caps and tech are confirming broad risk-on breadth rather than a narrow sector call. The distribution is moderately top-heavy: 3 names above 80, 7 above 65, median at 71, with VNQ (63) the lone Watch-tier name below consensus. Against a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11), the dual-momentum signals are valid but carry no regime tailwind, so position sizing should reflect that ambiguity. First preference is EEM at moderate conviction, given its clean score lead, with IWM as the domestic companion if the risk-on read holds.

🧠 Strips SPY beta from 12-month returns and ranks the residual to isolate idiosyncratic momentum independent of the broad market.
Construction
Blitz residual momentum: stock 12m return after removing market beta (regress returns on SPY, rank the residual).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where IV30 minus HV20 exceeds 1.5 standard deviations on a 90-day z-score, identifying overstated fear premiums for premium selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $GLD $NKE $CMCSA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1GLD ↓78● Signal$385.35 -0.25%50%26%1.97FEAR
2NKE ↓65● Signal$45.65 -0.69%55%34%1.63FEAR
3CMCSA ↓63 Watch$24.12 +0.65%36%23%1.59FEAR
4UVXY ↓62 Watch$30.20 -1.98%96%62%1.56FEAR
5VXX ↓62 Watch$25.01 -1.22%65%42%1.56FEAR
6XLC ↓62 Watch$112.02 -0.09%19%12%1.56FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (WR 53.2%, avg +7.5% vs SPY +3.6%) confirms a real but modest alpha edge, yet the monthly cohort trend is softening, April averaged 10.8% and May dropped to 4.9%, and with the formal backtest still unvalidated at N=25, the 4.0-point SPY spread is the single number anchoring whatever conviction this screener currently earns.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 53% · avg +7.5% · beats SPY +3.6% by +4.0 pts
Last 90 days: 276 signals captured · 156 resolved at +20d · WR 53% · avg +7.5% · vs SPY +3.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 227 closed · WR TB 48% · 3% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

COST, LLY, and TSLA are the only three names at 100 (Very strong), but all carry null pattern tags, so the thesis rests entirely on the vol spread with no directional confirmation. The remaining five names cluster between 69 and 73, making this batch bifurcated rather than broadly strong, with a median of 71 across 12 signals and all 12 clearing the 65 floor. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) neither hurts nor helps premium selling, leaving execution quality as the main variable. TSLA at 100 offers the fattest premium but the most volatile realized vol history, which cuts both ways for short-gamma positioning. Prefer COST first at moderate conviction, given more stable realized vol and a cleaner premium-selling profile than either LLY or TSLA without pattern data to differentiate them.

Acquirer's Multiple (deep value)

ACQUIRERS_MULTIPLEEODData: 2026-06-13
Live: acquirers_multiple_screener.py (Carlisle EV/EBIT factor)

STARTER · untested
no backtest stats yet
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Ranks non-financial, non-utility stocks by EV/EBIT ascending, surfacing cheapest operating earners where mean-reversion in valuation drives the edge.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $UHS $HBB $VRRM
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayAcquirer's MultipleEVOperating EarningsMarket CapEV source
1UHS ↑100●●● Very strong$146.00 -0.03%7.03$14.01B$1.99B$9.34Byfinance
2HBB ↑88●● Strong$20.32 +0.44%8.41$0.31B$0.04B$0.27Byfinance
3VRRM ↑75● Signal$4.46 -0.78%9.30$1.76B$0.19B$0.58Byfinance
4ARLP ↑62 Watch$25.07 -0.26%9.68$3.73B$0.39B$3.21Byfinance
5ORGO ↑50 Watch$2.39 +0.47%9.71$0.43B$0.04B$0.34Byfinance
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

UHS leads at score 100 (Very strong), the lone name the model treats as deeply cheap on an operating-earnings basis today, making it the clear first look. HBB follows at 88 (Strong), a small-cap household appliance name that rarely surfaces near the top of a value sort, which adds interest. VRRM at 75 clears the Signal threshold but sits in the middle of a thin, top-heavy batch of only five names with a median of 75 and just two names above 80. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 60.11) is not an outright headwind for deep value but provides no tailwind either, so the setup is cautious. UHS is the first-look long at moderate conviction, with HBB as a secondary idea if the position-size appetite exists for small-cap liquidity risk.

🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day relative strength vs SPY plus sector breadth and cluster position, surfacing momentum leaders inside rotating sectors.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.68 +0.86%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$77.73 +0.04%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$5.29 +0.38%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$129.72 +0.13%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$88.00 +1.20%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags retail-driven melt-ups via social attention z-score, call/put skew, and price stretch without an earnings catalyst, targeting mean reversion from crowded momentum.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $AMKR $AEO $ANF
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1AMKR ↓100●●● Very strong$75.95 -0.26%FOMO1.00-0.3%
2AEO ↓77● Signal$18.70 +2.24%EUPHORIA0.772.2%
3ANF ↓62 Watch$92.91 +2.53%GREED0.622.5%
4ASTS ↓51 Watch$92.24 -5.45%GREED0.51-5.5%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (65% WR, avg +1.3%, beats SPY by 1.3 pts, KEEP) looks functional, but the monthly cohort swing from April's -15.9% avg and 17% WR to May's +3.4% and 73% WR flags extreme regime sensitivity that the aggregate number flatters.

AMKR prints a perfect 100 and lands in Very strong tier, but that tier's 90-day avg return is -1.24%, the worst of any tier by that measure, so the screener's top score does not map cleanly to best outcome. ANF at 62 in Watch tier is the quieter candidate worth attention, because Watch (n=18) is the only tier combining a 67% WR with a positive avg of +1.67%. The batch is thin overall - 4 names, median 62, one signal above 80 - and a NEUTRAL macro regime at 60.11 provides no directional tailwind for short setups. First preference is ANF at low conviction, not AMKR, because the tier history matters more than the headline score.

Composite ranking of HRHR-style options trades: combines best confluence (e.g. SMART_FLOW × UNUSUAL_V3), Sharpe/WR of the combo, IV rank, IV/HV spread, days-to-earnings, 52w position, regime multiplier.

Put/Call Skew Spike

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes above a 90-day baseline as a contrarian long signal when underlying price action is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $ORCL $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1ORCL ↑72● Signal$183.24 -0.47%56%640.02BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
2AVGO ↑57 Watch$379.52 -1.57%48%730.05BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (45 resolved, WR 56%, avg +15.1%, beating SPY by 12.3 points) confirms the KEEP verdict and the May cohort improving to WR 60% and avg +23.4% from April's WR 47% and +9.2% suggests a strengthening edge, but the tier inversion - higher-conviction tiers posting negative average returns while Watch tier runs at +26.7% - is the number that demands explanation before sizing up.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 56% · avg +15.1% · beats SPY +2.8% by +12.3 pts
Last 90 days: 100 signals captured · 45 resolved at +20d · WR 56% · avg +15.1% · vs SPY +2.8%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 73 closed · WR TB 38% · 4% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV crushes call IV above the 90th percentile, surfacing unusual institutional hedging or directional put demand.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$379.52 -1.57%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape shows 6 resolved trades at 100% win rate and a 18.0% avg 20d return against SPY's 1.87%, which sounds compelling, but the verdict is WATCH precisely because 6 is a third of the 20-trade minimum needed for any statistical weight, and the backtest itself remains untested, so the strong recent numbers confirm nothing yet.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV is cheaper than Yang-Zhang 20-day realized vol, targeting vol underpricing before the catalyst.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $ADBE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1ADBE ↑50 Watch$197.04 -9.94%REVERSAL0UP (reversal)BUY CALL (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin to the point of being nearly unusable. One name, ADBE, clears the screen at a score of 50 and a Watch tier, with no pattern tag to anchor the setup. A single signal at the median possible score means the screener found no conviction candidate, only a marginal one. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 60.11, which does not help or hurt a vol-long thesis, but a straddle on ADBE at Watch-tier confidence in a directionless tape is low-priority. Hold off entirely until either ADBE re-scores above 65 or the screen produces at least three names with differentiated scores.
